stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking activity around a major LLM from Meta.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core field of large language models (LLMs) and text generation.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama architecture.

stories.title: In a bold move, Rocket Lab acquires Iridium Communications
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: Rocket Lab is acquiring Iridium Communications, signaling a major strategic move in the space sector.
The acquisition is described as potentially one of the most transformative deals in the space industry.
This merger combines launch capabilities (Rocket Lab) with global satellite communication infrastructure (Iridium).

stories.title: Agent-Native Immune System: Architecture, Taxonomy, and Engineering
stories.author: Dehui Li
bullet_points: The shift from static chatbots to autonomous agents introduces a significantly expanded AI threat landscape.
Existing defense mechanisms (like perimeter security and training alignment) are inadequate because they operate outside the agent's active reasoning loop.
New vulnerabilities include runtime hijacking via memory poisoning, tool-chain manipulation, and multi-agent attacks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1 model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its focus on generative AI capabilities.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: DeepSeek-V4-Pro is trending on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher quality outputs, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra, a new large language model.
The model is specifically engineered for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is designed to support complex, long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for building personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run entirely on user hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, integrating local LLM execution into the agent ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads on Apple devices.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and adoption.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running all AI processes entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: WhatsApp is launching usernames: here’s how to reserve yours
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: WhatsApp is introducing usernames, allowing users to communicate without sharing their phone number.
The feature aims to enhance user privacy by concealing phone numbers from non-contacts.
The rollout is scheduled for 'later this year'.

stories.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
stories.author: Hayden Field
bullet_points: Lawmakers are proposing a ban on selling Americans' health and location data to data brokers.
The proposed legislation would specifically cover information shared with AI chatbots like ChatGPT and Claude.
Senator Elizabeth Warren and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon are spearheading the new Health and Location bill.

stories.title: Google warns EU's plans to weaken its monopoly could expose user data
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is warning against EU regulatory plans aimed at breaking its monopoly.
The EU is pushing for Google to share search data and open up AI features on Android.
Google's primary objection centers on significant potential risks to user privacy.

stories.title: NASA's X-59 "frankenjet" tests supersonic flight without the sonic boom
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: NASA is conducting tests on the X-59 aircraft to achieve supersonic flight without generating a traditional sonic boom.
This technology represents a significant advancement in aerospace engineering, potentially revolutionizing supersonic travel.
The successful development could allow for future commercial supersonic flights to operate without major noise restrictions.

stories.title: Govern the Repository, Not the Agent: Measuring Ecosystem-Level Risk in AI-Native Software
stories.author: Daniel Russo
bullet_points: Autonomous coding agents are now operating at scale within shared software repositories.
Current evaluation methods focus on individual agent performance rather than ecosystem-level risk.
The research introduces 'integration friction' to measure cumulative problems arising from multiple agent contributions.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: The influx of AI-assisted scientific discovery is outpacing the capacity of traditional human peer review.
The paper proposes using AI tools, such as Google's Paper Assistant, to automate and accelerate the scientific review process.
The research introduces a taxonomy to frame the necessary transition toward AI-driven verification in science.

stories.title: Vision-Default, Prior-Override: Causal Mechanisms of Perception-Knowledge Conflict in Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Michal Golovanevsky
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical problem of how Vision-Language Models (VLMs) resolve conflicts between visual input and pre-existing world knowledge.
It moves beyond behavioral characterization by providing a component-level, causal account of this perception-knowledge conflict.
The study employs advanced mechanistic analysis techniques, including activation patching across different model granularities (attention heads, MLP sublayers, etc.).

stories.title: Mechanism-Driven Monitors for Preemptive Detection of LLM Training Instability
stories.author: Yang Zheng
bullet_points: Addresses a critical, costly problem in LLM training: the delayed detection of instability failures.
Proposes a novel solution using 'mechanism-driven monitors' derived from the functional role of model modules.
Aims to enable preemptive detection of training faults before they cause catastrophic failures in massive compute runs.

stories.title: Ornith-1.0: self-improving open-source models for agentic coding
stories.author: danboarder
bullet_points: Introduction of Ornith-1.0, a new open-source model focused on agentic coding.
The model features self-improving capabilities, suggesting advanced autonomy in software development.
The open-source nature positions it as a potentially significant contribution to the AI/ML community for coding tasks.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The new feature set includes the capability for web search functionality in Claude Code.
This enhancement suggests a move towards more complex, autonomous AI workflows.

stories.title: Sony’s next-gen PlayStation will go ‘beyond the living room’
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Sony is planning a next-generation PlayStation experience that extends beyond the traditional living room setup.
The platform aims to address the migration of users from the PlayStation ecosystem to gaming PCs.
The announcement was made during a Q&A session with investors, suggesting a strategic focus on platform expansion.

stories.title: These camera-free smart glasses made me feel like Tony Stark
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Xgimi, a projector company, is entering the smart glasses market with the MemoMind One.
The glasses feature a privacy-focused design by omitting cameras, differentiating them from Meta/Snap AR glasses.
The product debuted at CES 2026 and emphasizes a lighter, more discreet user experience.

stories.title: Comcast is splitting its media and broadband properties
stories.author: Oliver Barnes and Daniel Thomas, Financial Times
bullet_points: Comcast is planning a major corporate restructuring by splitting its business units.
The media division, NBCUniversal, and the broadband properties will be separated.
Sky will also be spun off into its own independent company.

stories.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: DexCompose addresses the challenge of composing individual dexterous manipulation policies into multi-task skills using a single hand.
The core problem addressed is destructive interference caused by conflicting demands on overlapping fingers when adding new tasks to existing skills.
The proposed solution is a role-aware residual composition framework designed to effectively reuse pretrained dexterous policies.

stories.title: Agentic Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution
stories.author: Brucek Khailany
bullet_points: Introduces HORIZON, a self-evolving agent framework for hardware design.
Treats hardware design processes as analogous to repository-level code evolution, utilizing Git operations for state management.
The system uses a Markdown harness to encapsulate domain knowledge, evaluation logic, and policy for hands-free agent iteration.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: AI is accelerating scientific discovery, leading to an influx of AI-assisted research.
Traditional human peer review systems are struggling to keep pace with this rapid acceleration.
The paper proposes using AI tools, like Google's Paper Assistant, to automate and accelerate the scientific review process.

stories.title: How Width and Data Shape Generalization Scaling Laws in Quadratic Neural Networks
stories.author: Lenka Zdeborová
bullet_points: The paper investigates joint generalization scaling laws in Quadratic Neural Networks (QNNs) concerning model width and data shape.
It addresses limitations in existing scaling law theories, which often focus on fixed-feature or infinite-width regimes.
The research specifically analyzes how generalization scales with the number of trainable parameters and the number of samples in a feature-learning model.

stories.title: Exposure Bias Can Alleviate Itself via Directional and Frequency Rectification in Flow Matching
stories.author: Shao-Lun Huang
bullet_points: The paper addresses exposure bias in Flow Matching (FM) generative models, a known issue stemming from training/inference discrepancies.
It proposes DEFAR (DirEctional-Frequency Adaptive Rectification), a novel framework that uses the bias's inherent dynamic signals for self-correction.
This approach offers a dynamic alternative to existing static or heuristic methods for mitigating exposure bias in generative AI.

stories.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand
stories.author: Mingyu Ding
bullet_points: DexCompose addresses the challenge of composing individual dexterous manipulation policies into multi-task skills using a single hand.
The core problem addressed is destructive interference caused by conflicting demands on overlapping fingers when adding new tasks to existing skills.
The proposed solution is a role-aware residual composition framework designed to effectively reuse pretrained dexterous policies.

stories.title: VGB for Masked Diffusion Model: Efficient Test-time Scaling for Reward Satisfaction and Sample Editing
stories.author: Molei Tao
bullet_points: Introduces MDM-VGB, a discrete diffusion sampler designed for Masked Diffusion Models (MDM).
Focuses on efficient test-time scaling to ensure generated outputs satisfy structural constraints or optimize rewards.
Leverages reward-guided remasking, drawing inspiration from backtracking Markov chain methods for reward-tilted generation.

stories.title: Democratic ICAI: Debating Our Way to Steering Principles from Preferences
stories.author: Shirish Karande
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of preference-based alignment methods in capturing the underlying reasoning behind human judgments.
It focuses on improving Inverse Constitutional AI (ICAI) to provide more nuanced and comprehensive explanations for complex decisions.
The research aims to move beyond simple pairwise labels to summarize complex preferences into natural-language steering principles.

stories.title: Towards Automating Scientific Review with Google's Paper Assistant Tool
stories.author: Vincent Cohen-Addad
bullet_points: The paper addresses the scalability crisis in scientific peer review due to the rapid rise of AI-assisted research.
It proposes using AI tools, like Google's Paper Assistant, to automate and accelerate the scientific verification and review process.
The authors introduce a taxonomy to frame the necessary transition toward AI-driven scientific validation.

stories.title: HPRO: Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization via Preference Extraction for Emotional Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Xiangmin Xu
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of standard LLM-based Text-to-Speech (TTS) models, which often produce statistically averaged prosody lacking emotional expressiveness.
It introduces HPRO (Hierarchical Progressive Reward Optimization) as a solution to enhance emotional TTS quality.
HPRO specifically tackles structural mismatches in preference-driven optimization, such as information conflict between content and emotion in the latent space.

stories.title: Cognitive Episodes in LLM Reasoning Traces Enable Interpretable Human Item Difficulty Prediction
stories.author: Dawei Zhou
bullet_points: The research proposes a novel approach to predicting human item difficulty in educational assessments using LLM reasoning traces.
It shifts the focus from static item text representations to observable cognitive processes induced by the problem-solving burden.
This method aims to reduce reliance on expensive human calibration for creating fairer and more effective tests.

stories.title: From Tokens to States: LLMs as a Special Case of World Models and the Continuous Path Beyond
stories.author: Paul Dubois
bullet_points: The paper challenges the binary view separating LLMs (token predictors) and World Models (reality simulators).
It proposes that LLMs can be viewed as a degenerate special case of World Models, where the state space is defined by token sequences.
The work aligns with arguments (like LeCun's) suggesting that achieving general intelligence may require moving beyond purely autoregressive token prediction.

stories.title: MultiHashFormer: Hash-based Generative Language Models
stories.author: Nikolaos Aletras
bullet_points: Introduces MultiHashFormer, a new framework for hash-based generative language models.
Addresses the limitation of prior hashing methods (many-to-one collisions) in causal LMs.
Allows for hash-based autoregression by representing each token as a unique hash signature.

stories.title: Can LLMs Judge Better Than They Generate? Evaluating Task Asymmetry, Mechanistic Interpretability and Transferability for In-Context QA
stories.author: Sambaran Bandyopadhyay
bullet_points: The paper challenges the common assumption that LLM evaluation (LLM-as-a-Judge) is inherently easier than generation.
It tests this asymmetry in a controlled in-context QA setting, isolating the evaluation task from open-domain parametric knowledge.
Findings show that generation accuracy surpassed self-evaluation accuracy on three out of four tested benchmarks.

stories.title: Qwen 3.6 27B is the sweet spot for local development
stories.author: stared
bullet_points: Qwen 3.6 27B model is highlighted as an optimal choice for local AI development.
The discussion centers on the practical usability and performance of this specific large language model (LLM).
This indicates a trend towards more accessible, powerful models for on-premise or local deployment.
